differential_diagnoses: Portal hypertensive biliopathy leading to hemobilia
Ectopic variceal bleeding (jejunal or stomal varices)
Portal vein thrombosis with secondary portal cavernoma
Arteriovenous malformation/fistula at the hepaticojejunostomy site
Ischemic gastropathy or portal hypertensive gastropathy
next_steps: Interventional radiology consultation for diagnostic and potentially therapeutic portal venography
Direct visualization of the biliary tree via percutaneous transhepatic cholangiography (PTC) or endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP)
Balloon-occluded retrograde transvenous obliteration (BRTO) if ectopic varices are identified
Liver biopsy or brush cytology of the hilar lesion to rule out malignancy
Repeat EGD focusing on careful evaluation of the jejunostomy anastomosis
Multidisciplinary team review involving hepatobiliary surgery and interventional radiology
